ops.title: Faceless helper apps are fine, but every user-visible surface carries the product name, never an internal slug
ops.body:
Faceless (Dock-less, LSUIElement) helper apps are fine, but every user-visible surface — app name, icon, Homebrew cask token, notification identity — carries the product name (e.g. `captain-hook`, `Captain Hook.app`), never an internal slug like `capt-hook-helper`. Internal identifiers (bundle IDs, app groups, provisioning-profile names) stay as committed; they are invisible and TCC/validation-keyed.

**Why:** The user rejected a release plan whose cask was `capt-hook-helper`: "i just didnt want it being called 'capt-hook-helper' … its ok if it stays faceless just wanted it to have a proper name and icon" (2026-07-19, captain-hook first helper release).

**How to apply:** When naming casks, app bundles, menu-bar/notification identities, or anything a user sees in /Applications, brew, or System Settings, use the product name; keep internal slugs confined to bundle IDs, sockets, and paths. Precedent: cc-pool's app cask is `cc-pool-status`, captain-hook's is `captain-hook`. See [[verify-track-state-before-dispatch]] for the companion shipping lesson.
